mission.name: InterestShield July 2026 Rebuild
mission.tagline: Make the financial truth executable, then make the experience unforgettable.
mission.northStar: Truth-first, hope-forward Velocity Banking education with correct math, explicit assumptions, private owner-scoped data, calm coaching, and no promised outcomes.

policies.notes: Merge each focused task only after local gates and GitHub CI pass. Split work beyond roughly 400 changed implementation lines.

phases.title: Release Truth
phases.goal: The public Vercel alias and InterestShield custom domain serve the current main artifact and pass production smoke at desktop and mobile widths.

gates.title: Web regression and accessibility suite
Web production build
All seven local routes answer
Public production alias serves current markers
gates.notes: Public alias still serves dpl_FfPyuRhZM8G4pTofYifoajjVDpLg. Latest green target is dfb4d91 at velocity-banking-mvp-v2-78l3t4zuy-islanddevcrew.vercel.app.

tasks.title: Resolve Vercel authentication and confirm project ownership
tasks.notes: Vercel connector reports oauth_token_invalid_grant and CLI 55.0.0 has no local credentials. Verify which project owns web-islanddevcrew.vercel.app and the custom InterestShield domain after reauthentication.

tasks.title: Promote the latest green main artifact and attach production domains
tasks.notes: Promote without rebuilding, purge cache, preserve a documented rollback target.

tasks.title: Run production Browser and Chrome responsive smoke
tasks.notes: Verify freshness markers, four vitals, artifact rail, no console errors, and no offscreen controls at 375px.

phases.title: Calculation Contract v2
phases.goal: Every displayed balance is ledger-continuous and lender-dependent assumptions are explicit, versioned, and executable.

gates.title: Financial engine regression suite
gates.notes: 241 regressions plus two independent anonymized reference scenarios cover ledger invariants, lender-term boundaries, and transaction-calendar accrual.

gates.title: Web build consumes the versioned contract
Expo TypeScript consumes the shared contract

tasks.title: Fix canonical Money Loop ledger continuity
tasks.notes: Corrected LOC event balanceAfter semantics. Red evidence: ops/mission/evidence/P1-T1-red-2026-07-12.txt; green evidence: ops/mission/evidence/P1-G1-2026-07-12.txt.

tasks.title: Define lender terms and confidence contract
tasks.notes: Version fees, rate mode, draw and repayment periods, minimum draws, minimum payments, and missing-input confidence. Red evidence: ops/mission/evidence/P1-T2-red-2026-07-12.txt; green evidence: ops/mission/evidence/P1-G1-2026-07-12.txt.

tasks.title: Add transaction-calendar LOC accrual
tasks.notes: Model actual month length, paydays, bill dates, and daily closing balances with a labeled estimate fallback. Red evidence: ops/mission/evidence/P1-T3-red-2026-07-12.txt; green evidence: ops/mission/evidence/P1-G1-2026-07-12.txt.

tasks.title: Add lender fixture and independent reference lane
tasks.notes: Use anonymized fixtures and compare core outputs against closed forms or an independent reference implementation. Red evidence: ops/mission/evidence/P1-T4-red-2026-07-12.txt; green evidence: ops/mission/evidence/P1-G1-2026-07-12.txt.

phases.title: Supabase Foundation
phases.goal: Web and Expo can persist private, owner-scoped, recoverable, deletable financial state through one audited data contract.

gates.title: Supabase local schema and RLS integration tests
gates.notes: Static contract, 21 pgTAP owner-isolation/idempotency tests, and isolated backup/restore drill pass; PostgreSQL 17 schema lint and security advisors remain clean.

gates.title: Web regression suite with persistence adapters
gates.notes: Web 243 regressions, account and adapter contracts, independent fixtures, accessibility, lint, build, seven-route HTTP smoke, and Chrome responsive smoke pass.

gates.title: Expo shared persistence types compile
gates.notes: Expo adapter and account/deep-link contracts, TypeScript, SDK compatibility, seven-route web export smoke, and Android Hermes bundle export pass.

gates.title: Cloudflare report Worker security and dry-run bundle
gates.command: npm --prefix apps/report-worker run check && npm --prefix apps/report-worker test && npm --prefix apps/report-worker run build:dry-run
gates.notes: Seven Worker behavior/security tests, generated bindings, zero dependency findings, and deployment dry-run pass with one R2 binding and no D1 binding.

tasks.title: Repair and apply the owner-scoped Supabase schema
tasks.notes: Align profile ownership, add owner-consistent foreign keys, and use least-privilege grants. Red evidence: ops/mission/evidence/P2-T1-red-2026-07-12.txt; green evidence: ops/mission/evidence/P2-G1-2026-07-12.txt.

tasks.title: Adversarially test RLS, Data API grants, and deletion
tasks.notes: Test anonymous, owner A, owner B, update ownership, exports, audit events, and account deletion. Red evidence: ops/mission/evidence/P2-T2-red-2026-07-12.txt; green evidence: ops/mission/evidence/P2-G1-2026-07-12.txt.

tasks.title: Wire web and Expo authentication and snapshot sync
tasks.notes: Database idempotency PR #224, shared mutation contract #225, web adapter #226, Expo SecureStore adapter #228, web account UI #229, and Expo account/deep-link UI #230 are merged. Ownership comes from verified claims and no service credential enters either client.

tasks.title: Prove backup, restore, retention, and incident logging
tasks.notes: PR #231 proves a two-archive synthetic restore with all six owned tables, RLS and policy counts, cleanup, retention schedule, deletion boundaries, and incident procedure. Managed provider restore remains a production project gate.

tasks.title: Add selective Cloudflare export and report lane
tasks.notes: PR #232 adds an authenticated Worker + private R2 report-object lane. Supabase remains the sole financial handoff target and system of record; no D1 mirror or live Cloudflare resource was created.

phases.title: Web Product Overhaul
phases.goal: The site and dashboard are calm, fast, accessible, modular, and centered on four truthful vitals plus the Money Loop experience.

gates.title: Web tests and accessibility contract
gates.notes: Web 244 regressions, two independent fixtures, accessibility, metadata, and public asset contracts pass.

gates.title: Web lint
gates.notes: ESLint passes across the asset cleanup, responsive tab fix, and browser automation.

gates.title: Web production build
gates.notes: Next.js 16.2.10 builds 14 static pages from the final 0.13 MiB public asset set.

gates.title: Seven-route rendered smoke and responsive overflow
gates.notes: Playwright-core checks all seven routes at 1440x900 and 390x844 plus the Money Loop LOC interaction against a fresh production build, with an explicit wait for client-mounted route markers.

tasks.title: Freeze information architecture and dashboard first viewport
tasks.notes: PR #233 delivers the compact four-vital instrument strip, model-backed Money Loop stage, graphite shell, chrome-free domain scroller, and desktop/mobile width containment. Red evidence: ops/mission/evidence/P3-T1-red-2026-07-12.txt.

tasks.title: Split oversized Learn, Portfolio, and mobile shell modules
tasks.notes: PR #234 reduces Learn, Portfolio, and Expo shell orchestration to 494, 63, and 207 lines with bounded modules. Both GitHub quality runs and Vercel passed after the root mobile-port contract was updated to follow extracted ownership.

tasks.title: Replace emoji navigation and complete metadata surfaces
tasks.notes: PR #235 delivers Lucide shell icons plus route-specific metadata, manifest, robots, sitemap, and generated social image. Both GitHub quality runs and Vercel passed after route smoke was aligned with route-specific titles.

tasks.title: Compress the 79.8 MiB visual library and enforce budgets
tasks.notes: PR #236 removed 79.69 MiB of unreachable visuals, converted the live Guardian to 28.6 KiB WebP, enforced reference-aware asset budgets, and added fresh-build rendered smoke that found and fixed a real 390px simulator overflow and a Linux hydration race.

phases.title: Model-Driven 3D Experience
phases.goal: A single performant Three.js show stage lets users select five data-bound artifacts with accessible controls and graceful fallbacks.

gates.title: Web tests
gates.notes: Current main c7b9705 passes 263 regressions plus fixtures, strict PNG and lifecycle helpers, accessibility, metadata, and assets.

gates.title: 3D rendered, interaction, and nonblank canvas checks
gates.notes: Current main c7b9705 passes a fresh build plus full/efficient all-five pixel, motion, focus, responsive, adversarial hidden-WebGL, and reduced-motion fallback lanes.

gates.title: Web production build within asset budgets
gates.notes: Current main c7b9705 passes a separate 14-page production build after the merged visual gate.

tasks.title: Define the five-artifact visual grammar and data bindings
tasks.notes: PR #237 binds five canonical geometries to normalized model channels, explicit operational state, fail-closed runtime validation, canonical DOM fallback, and static/efficient/full capability policy. Both GitHub quality runs and Vercel passed after three adversarial review rounds closed every finding.

tasks.title: Build the lazy-loaded Three.js selection stage
tasks.notes: PR #238 delivers the client-only procedural stage, all five contract geometries, DOM-owned mesh selection, 650 ms demand-rendered settlement, static first paint/fallback, and production-consumed runtime contracts. Both GitHub quality runs and Vercel passed after spec and quality approval.

tasks.title: Add reduced motion, static fallback, and mobile GPU controls
tasks.notes: PR #239 delivers fail-closed static/efficient/full policy, WebGL2 probe and renderer startup/loss paths, DPR/geometry budgets, offscreen/hidden pause, first-frame reveal, and runtime-tested cleanup. Both GitHub quality runs and Vercel passed.

tasks.title: Verify screenshots, pixels, interaction, performance, and accessibility
tasks.notes: PR #240 merged at c7b9705 after duplicate GitHub Ubuntu quality runs and Vercel passed. Fresh main reruns prove all-five isolated pixels, under-700-ms settlement, DOM motion, responsive geometry, static fallback, focus order, adversarial failure lanes, and the 14-page build.

phases.title: Educational Media Pipeline
phases.goal: HyperFrames and Remotion produce deterministic, branded explainers without entering the calculator runtime bundle.

gates.title: Media lint, validation, overflow, and contrast
gates.notes: P5-T1 identity, contrast-pair, deterministic timing, financial semantics, runtime-boundary, document-sync, and CI checks pass on the task branch. Gate remains pending until T2/T3 add real HyperFrames and Remotion overflow, contrast, and composition validation.

gates.title: Representative Remotion still render

tasks.title: Create the InterestShield visual identity and motion contract
tasks.notes: Ready for PR on codex/mission/p5-visual-identity-contract: renderer-neutral identity version 1, portable timing/seed vectors, fail-closed LOC display channels, three format/caption contracts, anti-patterns, package-family isolation, synchronized human specification, and 14 focused tests are independently approved. Evidence: ops/mission/evidence/P5-T1-visual-identity-contract-2026-07-13.txt.

tasks.title: Build HyperFrames launch and tutorial compositions
tasks.notes: Use deterministic GSAP timelines, transitions, captions, contrast, and overflow checks.

tasks.title: Build Remotion scenario explainers from engine JSON
tasks.notes: Render baseline, first Money Loop month, and blocked-plan stories in 16:9, 9:16, and square.

phases.title: Expo Native Hardening
phases.goal: The existing Expo app becomes a tested native product with shared-engine parity, secure persistence, and Android/iOS release evidence.

gates.title: Expo TypeScript
Expo web export and route smoke
gates.notes: Run build:web first when dist-web is stale.

gates.title: Android bundle and emulator smoke
iOS bundle and simulator smoke through macOS CI
gates.notes: Requires the existing macOS GitHub Actions lane or another macOS host.

tasks.title: Split the monolithic mobile shell into native route modules
tasks.notes: Preserve Expo Router and shared financial-engine ownership.

tasks.title: Wire native auth, SecureStore, sync, and offline recovery
tasks.notes: Use Expo Go first and add a development build only for a demonstrated native requirement.

tasks.title: Complete native accessibility and responsive UI
tasks.notes: Safe areas, tab semantics, screen readers, reduced motion, and selectable financial data.

tasks.title: Prove Android and iOS release candidates
tasks.notes: Record device and simulator evidence, bundle checks, deep links, and shared-engine fixtures.

risks.title: Vercel authentication and alias ownership are unresolved
risks.note: GitHub issue #59; the public URL still serves a stale deployment.

risks.title: Lender-specific terms are discussed but not executable
risks.note: Do not persist or market lender-specific projections until P1 closes.

risks.title: No dedicated InterestShield Supabase cloud project exists
risks.note: The repaired migration passes local PostgreSQL 17, lint, and security advisors, but no dedicated InterestShield cloud project exists yet.

risks.title: Cloudflare report Worker has no live R2 resources or deployment
risks.note: The Worker passes tests and deployment dry-run but intentionally refuses placeholder configuration; create private buckets and deploy only after explicit resource/cost approval.

risks.title: Expo SDK 56 inherits moderate CLI and config audit advisories
risks.note: npm reports ten moderate findings in Expo CLI/config tooling and proposes an invalid Expo 46 downgrade; track upstream SDK updates and do not force the downgrade.

risks.title: iOS simulator smoke requires macOS
risks.note: Use the existing GitHub Actions macOS lane when P6 becomes active.

prLog.title: Add shared snapshot sync mutation contract
Add verified web Supabase snapshot adapter
Add web account and private snapshot sync
Add Expo account and private snapshot sync
Prove Supabase backup and restore recovery
Add semantic icons and metadata surfaces
Enforce asset budgets and rendered browser smoke
Define the Money Loop 3D visual contract
Add the lazy procedural Money Loop Three.js stage
Verify the Money Loop Three.js experience

nextActions: Publish and merge P5-T1 from codex/mission/p5-visual-identity-contract after GitHub CI and Vercel pass, then rerun the media identity check on current main.

Start P5-T2 on a fresh branch: add the HyperFrames workspace and launch/tutorial compositions against identity contract version 1, with deterministic GSAP timelines, captions, contrast, overflow, and animation-map gates.
When Vercel reauthentication becomes available, return to P0-T1, confirm project and domain ownership, promote the verified main artifact, and rerun P0-G4 plus Browser and Chrome smoke.
blockers: P0-T1 is externally blocked: the Vercel connector requires reauthentication and CLI 55.0.0 has no local credentials; the latest deployment is protected.
The InterestShield custom-domain hostname is not recorded in the repository and must be discovered from Vercel project state.
conventions: Use codex/mission/<phase>-<task> branches and one focused PR per task.
Write a failing regression test before every behavior fix and record red/green evidence.
Keep financial calculations in packages/financial-engine and share them with web and Expo.
Never hand-edit ops/mission/state-of-the-union.html; render it from state.json.
